skill.name: x-article-publisher
skill.description: Convert Markdown articles into rich text drafts for X Articles. The skill parses a local Markdown file, prepares cover and inline images, copies formatted content through the system clipboard, and guides browser automation for draft creation.
skill.summary: Create X Articles drafts from local Markdown files with rich text and image placement.
skill.author: wshuyi

security_audit.summary: Most static findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, documented Claude skill paths, and prior evaluation text in EVALUATION.json. Human review found one real medium-risk issue: Markdown is converted to HTML without escaping or URL sanitization before clipboard paste into X Articles. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, destructive commands, or confirmed malicious intent were found.

medium_findings.title: Unescaped Markdown Converted to Clipboard HTML
medium_findings.description: The Markdown parser converts user-controlled Markdown into HTML with regex substitutions and no HTML escaping. Link URLs are inserted directly into href attributes, and generated HTML is later copied to the system clipboard for pasting into X Articles. This is a content integrity risk if a malicious Markdown file contains unsafe HTML or crafted links.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parser directly transforms local Markdown into HTML and the clipboard script copies that HTML as rich text. The risk is clear, but impact depends on the source Markdown and X editor sanitization.

low_findings.title: Local File Reads and Clipboard Writes Are Broad but Expected
low_findings.description: The scripts read local Markdown, image, and HTML files from user-provided paths and write image or HTML data to the system clipboard. This matches the skill purpose, but users should understand that selected local content can be placed on the clipboard.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file and clipboard operations are explicit in the scripts and required for the workflow. I did not find evidence of hidden collection, persistence, or network exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Alerts
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance findings point to YAML text, argparse setup, Markdown title parsing, and the prior EVALUATION.json report. These are false positives, not cryptographic operations or system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited script lines are argparse or Markdown parsing logic, and SKILL.md line 3 is frontmatter text. EVALUATION.json is a prior report, not executable skill code.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static External Command Alerts in Documentation
low_findings.description: The external command findings in SKILL.md are Markdown inline code or fenced examples that document commands and browser-tool steps. They do not execute by themselves, but the workflow does intentionally ask the assistant to run local Python scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are visible Markdown examples or inline code markers. The only actual commands are expected Python script invocations for this publishing workflow.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Network URL Alerts
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to the X Articles compose page, which is the declared target service. No separate outbound request or hidden destination was found in the scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The SKILL.md URLs are browser navigation instructions for x.com. The EVALUATION.json alert is inside a prior audit report and is not executable code.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Hidden Path and Temp Directory Alerts
low_findings.description: The hidden-directory findings reference the documented Claude skill installation path. The temp-directory findings reference intermediate HTML files under /tmp, which are part of the local conversion workflow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are documented local workflow paths and not persistence locations. The risk is operational exposure of local files, which is covered separately as a low finding.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Obfuscation Alert
low_findings.description: The high-entropy alert on SKILL.md appears to be caused by normal Markdown documentation, mixed English and Chinese text, and many code examples. The file is readable plain text and not encrypted or packed content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review showed readable Markdown from the first line onward. I did not find binary payloads, encoded scripts, or concealed instructions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Regex HTML Generation Without Sanitization
dangerous_patterns.description: Markdown content is converted to HTML with regular expressions, including links and inline formatting, without escaping existing HTML or validating link schemes. This pattern can preserve unsafe content in the generated rich-text payload.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code directly constructs HTML strings from Markdown captures. The dangerous pattern is confirmed, though final platform sanitization may reduce exploitability.

content.user_title: Publish Markdown Drafts to X Articles
content.value_statement: Publishing long-form posts from Markdown to X Articles is slow and formatting can break. This skill converts local Markdown, prepares images, and guides draft creation for manual review.
actual_capabilities: Parses a local Markdown file and extracts a title, cover image, inline images, and article body.
Converts supported Markdown formatting into HTML for rich text clipboard paste.
Copies HTML or image data to the system clipboard on macOS or Windows.
Guides browser automation to open the X Articles editor and create a draft.
Uses block indexes to place inline images after the intended text blocks.
Keeps publishing manual by saving a draft for user review.
limitations: Requires a logged-in X account with access to X Articles.
Only supports local Markdown files and local image paths.
Generated HTML is not sanitized before clipboard paste.
Linux clipboard support is not implemented in the included script.

use_cases.title: Draft Technical Articles
use_cases.description: Convert Markdown tutorials into X Articles drafts while preserving headings, lists, links, and image order.

use_cases.title: Repurpose Blog Posts
use_cases.description: Move existing Markdown blog content into X Articles without manually rebuilding formatting in the editor.

use_cases.title: Prepare Image-Heavy Posts
use_cases.description: Place multiple article images using block indexes so each image appears near the intended paragraph.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for one local article.
prompt_templates.prompt: Publish /path/to/article.md to X Articles as a draft.

prompt_templates.title: Preview Parsed Content
prompt_templates.scenario: Check extracted title and image positions before browser work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Parse /path/to/article.md and show the title, cover image, and inline image positions.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Rich Text
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate clipboard-ready content without opening the browser.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert /path/to/article.md to rich text HTML and copy it to the clipboard.

prompt_templates.title: Full Publishing Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with manual validation and image placement.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an X Articles draft from /path/to/article.md, upload the cover image, paste the formatted body, insert inline images in reverse block order, then stop before publishing.

output_examples.input: Publish my Markdown tutorial to X Articles.
output: The article title was extracted from the first heading.
The first image was selected as the cover image.
The formatted body was copied to the clipboard for rich text paste.
The draft was prepared for manual review before publishing.

output_examples.input: Show image positions before creating the draft.
output: The cover image is separate from inline images.
Inline images have block positions for insertion after matching text sections.
Images should be inserted from the highest position to the lowest position.

output_examples.input: Convert my article body for X Articles only.
output: Supported Markdown formatting was converted for rich text paste.
Unsupported code block formatting was simplified to blockquote style.
The browser publishing step was not started.

best_practices: Review the generated X draft before publishing because converted HTML is not sanitized.
Use local Markdown files and image paths that you trust.
Insert inline images from highest block index to lowest block index.
anti_patterns: Do not use untrusted Markdown files without reviewing links and embedded HTML.
Do not expect the skill to publish automatically or bypass X account requirements.
Do not rely on text matching for image placement when block indexes are available.

faq.question: Does this skill publish automatically?
faq.answer: No. It creates and prepares a draft. The user must review and publish manually.

faq.question: Which platforms are supported?
faq.answer: The workflow targets X Articles. The clipboard script supports macOS and Windows.

faq.question: What Markdown features are supported?
faq.answer: It supports headings, paragraphs, bold text, links, blockquotes, and simple lists.

faq.question: How are images handled?
faq.answer: The first image becomes the cover image. Later images are inserted by block position.

faq.question: Is the generated HTML sanitized?
faq.answer: No. Review source Markdown and generated drafts before publishing.

faq.question: Does it work without X Premium access?
faq.answer: No. The X Articles editor requires an account with access to the Articles feature.
